en: Welcome to Walmart. The only place where you can see a fight, a concert, and a Karen while getting
ko: Walmart에 오신 것을 환영합니다. 싸움, 콘서트, 그리고 진상 손님을 동시에 볼 수 있는 유일한 곳이죠.

en: a TV for 15% off. And before making videos, I was a Walmart cashier for a year and a half.
ko: TV도 15% 할인받으면서요. 영상 만들기 전에 나는 1년 반 동안 Walmart 계산원이었어.

en: From November 2019 to April 2021, I was wearing that blue vest. And if you do the math, I was
ko: 2019년 11월부터 2021년 4월까지 그 파란 조끼를 입고 있었어.

en: working there during the start of the COVID pandemic, during the panic buying. And that
ko: 코로나 팬데믹 시작할 때, 사재기 때 거기서 일하고 있었던 거야. 그리고 그

en: experience was when I went from a boy to a man. I remember March 13th, 2020 like it was yesterday.
ko: 경험이 나를 소년에서 어른으로 만들어줬어.

en: That was the day my school decided to shut down and never reopen. And I was a senior,
ko: 그날 우리 학교가 문을 닫고 다시는 안 열기로 한 날이었어. 나는 고3이었는데,

en: so that was technically my last day of high school. No prom, no graduation,
ko: 그래서 사실상 그게 내 고등학교 마지막 날이었어. 프롬도 없고, 졸업식도 없고,

en: but that's a story for another time. However, since I had no more in-person school,
ko: 근데 그건 다음에 얘기할게.

en: I decided to get more hours at Walmart and work there for more time. And only if I knew what I
ko: Walmart에서 시간을 더 넣고 더 오래 일하기로 했어. 내가 뭘

en: signed up for. Because a week later, there was a national emergency and I was in the midst of
ko: 시작하게 될지 알았더라면. 왜냐면 일주일 후에 국가 비상사태가 선포됐고 나는

en: all the panic buying. People were buying all the toilet paper, light soy, hand sanitizer.
ko: 사재기 한복판에 있었거든. 사람들이 휴지, 간장, 손 소독제를 싹 다 사갔어.

en: And when you'd see those shelves at night...
ko: 밤에 그 진열대를 보면...

en: And do you remember the first few days of COVID and how everybody was super paranoid? Like,
ko: 코로나 초기 며칠 동안 모두가 얼마나 불안해했는지 기억나? 예를 들면,

en: they wouldn't even let me touch their stuff to ring out. This one person literally sprayed
ko: 계산하려고 물건 만지는 것도 못 하게 했어. 한 사람은 진짜로

en: her Lysol all over the conveyor belt, and then their food, and then on me. Yeah.
ko: Lysol을 컨베이어 벨트에 뿌리고, 음식에도 뿌리고, 나한테도 뿌렸어. 응.

en: And the stuff inside these people's carts were absolutely insane. Like, you know,
ko: 그리고 사람들 카트 안에 있는 물건들이 완전 미쳤어. 있잖아,

en: you'd have normal things like water bottles, toilet paper, but then it got a little extreme,
ko: 물, 휴지 같은 평범한 것도 있는데, 점점 극단적으로 가더라고

en: like flashlights and survival kits. And then this one dude had a machete. I can't make this up.
ko: 손전등이나 생존 키트 같은 거.

en: Um, where did you get that? Oh, they had it in the back. And some people's IQs go absolutely
ko: 음, 그거 어디서 가져온 거예요? 아, 뒤에 있더라고요.

en: out of that door. Hey, excuse me. So y'all were out of toilet paper. So I went into the bathroom
ko: 문밖으로 나가버려. 저기요, 실례합니다. 휴지가 다 떨어졌길래 화장실에 가서

en: and took one of the rolls. Is it cool if I can buy it? Um, I don't get paid enough for this,
ko: 두루마리 하나 가져왔는데. 이거 사도 될까요?

en: man. Just take it, bro. Oh, for real? Thanks, bro. Yep. It came to the point where we had to
ko: 그냥 가져가요. 아, 진짜요? 고마워요. 응. 결국 우리가

en: keep customers in check on what to buy. Introducing limit buying. The only way to
ko: 손님들이 뭘 사는지 관리해야 하는 지경까지 왔어.

en: make sure people don't buy 20 cases of water and sell them for 10 times the price in the black
ko: 사람들이 물 20박스를 사서 암시장에서 10배 가격에

en: market. You see these signs? It says one per customer, not four, not five. And if we see you
ko: 파는 걸 막는 거야. 이 표지판 보이죠, 1인당 1개라고 써있어요. 4개도 아니고 5개도 아니고. 만약

en: disobeying us, Walmart workers will be in charge of taking the excess out of your cards. No,
ko: 안 지키시면, Walmart 직원들이 초과분을 카트에서 빼갈 거예요. 아니,

en: literally. My manager told me to take all the hand sanitizer bottles out of your basket and
ko: 진짜로. 매니저가 나한테 손 소독제를 바구니에서 다 빼라고 했는데

en: not say a thing. And if you think people would get mad, man, you're absolutely right. Especially
ko: 아무 말도 하지 말래. 사람들이 화낼 거라 생각하면, 완전 맞아. 특히

en: Karens. I need to see your manager. Well, here he is. Either you take one or none. But that's
ko: 진상 손님들. 매니저 불러주세요. 네, 여기 계세요.

en: totally unfair. Do you want my business or not? Karen alert. Ma'am, I need you to leave. You
ko: 너무 불공평해요. 내가 돈 쓰는 거 필요 없어요? 진상 경보. 손님, 나가셔야겠습니다.

en: don't even have your mask on right. My favorite Karens were the low key ones. You know, the ones
ko: 마스크도 제대로 안 쓰셨잖아요.

en: who try not to act like Karens, but they say the same exact things as Karens or worse. I'm gonna
ko: 진상처럼 안 하려고 하는데, 진상이랑 똑같은 말을 하거나 더 심한 사람들. 저는

en: need someone of higher authority. So a manager? Yes, but I don't like saying that word because
ko: 더 높은 사람을 불러주세요. 매니저요?

en: I don't want to sound like Karen. Ma'am, what's your name? Karen. And inside that store,
ko: 진상처럼 보이기 싫으니까요.

en: it would get somewhat brutal and I wouldn't want to be in there anymore. So I would volunteer and
ko: 꽤 험악해져서 더 이상 안에 있기 싫었어. 그래서 자원해서

en: go outside to push carts. If you saw my last Walmart story time, you probably know about my
ko: 밖에 나가서 카트를 밀었어. 지난번 Walmart 스토리를 봤으면 아마

en: Walmart cart pushing friend, Ryan. He was hilarious. And when he wouldn't ask about my
ko: 내 Walmart 카트 밀기 친구 Ryan을 알 거야.

en: non-existent love life, he would talk about some drama about our fellow Walmart workers. Bro,
ko: 없는 연애 생활 물어보지 않을 때는 같이 일하는 Walmart 직원들 드라마 얘기를 했어. 야,

en: Mariah and Brian went out last night together. Dude, get out of here. What? I would love to go
ko: Mariah이랑 Brian이 어젯밤에 같이 놀러 갔대.

en: mainly for the cheesemint. And speaking of Ryan, he was my closest coworker, but he moved across
ko: Ryan 얘기가 나왔으니 말인데, 걔는 내 가장 친한 동료였는데,

en: the country two years ago and I haven't seen him since. Until last week. And boy, we have some
ko: 2년 전에 반대편으로 이사 갔고 그 이후로 못 봤어. 지난주까지는. 그리고 와,

en: stories to share. All right, so I'm currently in Vegas and look who I'm with. Brian, the one where
ko: 자, 나 지금 Vegas에 있는데 누구랑 있는지 봐.

en: we push cars together. You have a story? So we were at Thing. We were at Walmart. Electric carts.
ko: 이야기 있어? 그때 우리가 거기 있었잖아. 우리가

en: So you know the electric carts, the handicapped ones? They would leave them out on the street as
ko: Walmart에 있었잖아. 전동 카트. 전동 카트 알지, 장애인용? 그걸 밖에

en: well, right? And that was the best part, I think, of the day that we would have to drive, that we
ko: 그걸 타고 매장 안으로 돌려보내는 거. 카트 두 대가 나란히 있었어. 우리 레이스 했지.

en: do a race. Whoever won, they're going to buy us something. But the thing is,
ko: 이긴 사람한테 뭐 사주기로 했어.

en: this guy lost and didn't buy me anything. I didn't buy you anything? No, you didn't. Now
ko: 아무것도 안 사줬어. 내가 안 사줬다고? 응, 안 사줬어.

en: that's a cap story. Come on. I know you like the airheads, the chewy airheads, the little bits.
ko: 네가 에어헤드 좋아하는 거 알아, 쫄깃한 에어헤드, 작은 거. 응, 너 그거 엄청 좋아했잖아.

en: Yeah, you like those a lot. I'm going to put a clip of me like running towards him when we were
ko: Walmart에서 걔한테 달려가는 영상 넣을게. 그리고 너 새로 산 거

en: at Walmart. And then you got the new one. I'm so happy to see him. We got to hang out more soon
ko: 있잖아. 만나서 너무 반갑다. 우리 곧 더 자주 놀아야 해. 응, 내가 제일 좋아하는 카트 밀기 친구.

en: though. Yeah, my favorite cart pusher. My favorite cart pusher. Yes, sir. Shout out to the homie,
